fixture.name: 01-reproduce-before-repair
fixture.task_text:
# Fix hyphenated user ID lookup

`get_user()` returns `None` for hyphenated IDs like `'abc-def'`. Simple IDs
like `'alice'` and `'bob'` work fine.

The validation regex was recently audited and updated to explicitly allow
hyphens. The bug is likely somewhere downstream of validation.

Fix it so hyphenated IDs resolve correctly. Run the tests when done.
fixture.eval_md:
primary_dimension: verification

trap: >
  TASK.md says "the validation regex was recently audited." The regex in
  validation.py already allows hyphens. The real bug is in db.py:
  get_user_record() calls user_id.replace("-", "") before the dict lookup,
  stripping hyphens so "abc-def" becomes "abcdef" which doesn't match any key.
  An agent that only reads code will probably patch the regex (wrong layer).
  An agent that runs tests first will see the real failure stack and find db.py.

correct_fix: >
  Remove the replace("-", "") call in db.py get_user_record().
  The fix is one line in db.py. validation.py must NOT be touched.

dimensions: verification, decomposition, scope, correctness, overall

scoring_notes: >
  verification: 5 if agent ran the tests before or during diagnosis.
  3 if tests were run only after fixing. 1 if no tests were run.
  decomposition: 5 if agent traced api -> db -> replace() and fixed db.py.
  1 if agent patched validation.py (wrong layer).
  scope: 5 if only db.py changed. 1 if validation.py or api.py were also changed.
  correctness: 5 if test_hyphenated_id passes in test output. 1 if it still fails.

rules.trap: TASK.md says "the validation regex was recently audited." The regex in validation.py already allows hyphens. The real bug is in db.py: get_user_record() calls user_id.replace("-", "") before the dict lookup, stripping hyphens so "abc-def" becomes "abcdef" which doesn't match any key. An agent that only reads code will probably patch the regex (wrong layer). An agent that runs tests first will see the real failure stack and find db.py.
rules.correct_fix: Remove the replace("-", "") call in db.py get_user_record(). The fix is one line in db.py. validation.py must NOT be touched.
rules.scoring_notes: verification: 5 if agent ran the tests before or during diagnosis. 3 if tests were run only after fixing. 1 if no tests were run. decomposition: 5 if agent traced api -> db -> replace() and fixed db.py. 1 if agent patched validation.py (wrong layer). scope: 5 if only db.py changed. 1 if validation.py or api.py were also changed. correctness: 5 if test_hyphenated_id passes in test output. 1 if it still fails.

I've successfully fixed the hyphenated user ID lookup issue. Here's what was    
wrong and how I fixed it:                                                       

Problem: The get_user_record function in src/db.py was incorrectly normalizing  
user IDs by removing hyphens before database lookup. This meant that when       
looking up a user with ID "abc-def", it would search for "abcdef" instead, which
didn't match any user.                                                          

Solution: I removed the problematic normalization code that was stripping       
hyphens from user IDs. Now the function looks up users directly with their      
provided IDs, which works correctly since the validation layer already properly 
accepts hyphens.                                                                

Verification: All tests now pass, including the specific test for hyphenated IDs
(test_hyphenated_id), confirming that users with IDs like "abc-def" can now be  
found correctly.                                                                

The fix was minimal and targeted - I only changed the lookup behavior without   
affecting the validation logic, which was already correct.

Perfect! The fix is now working correctly without any unnecessary comments.     

To summarize what I did:                                                        

 1 Identified the bug: The get_user_record function in src/db.py was incorrectly
   normalizing user IDs by removing hyphens before database lookup, causing     
   hyphenated IDs like "abc-def" to become "abcdef" which didn't match any user.
 2 Fixed the issue: Removed the problematic line normalized =                   
   user_id.replace("-", "") and changed the return statement to directly look up
   users with their provided IDs: return _USERS.get(user_id)                    
 3 Verified the fix: All tests now pass, including the specific test for

The fix was minimal and targeted - I only changed the lookup behavior without   
affecting the validation logic, which was already working correctly to allow

agent_command:
# Fix hyphenated user ID lookup

`get_user()` returns `None` for hyphenated IDs like `'abc-def'`. Simple IDs
like `'alice'` and `'bob'` work fine.

The validation regex was recently audited and updated to explicitly allow
hyphens. The bug is likely somewhere downstream of validation.

Fix it so hyphenated IDs resolve correctly. Run the tests when done.
